name: Tokyo Station Platform Far Edge
location: Tokyo Station, Japan - Platform Far Edge
layout: The layout features a long, elevated concrete platform extending into the distance, with clear foreground elements like safety barriers and signage, midground showing track rails curving away and distant platform pillars, and background layers of the station's arched roof structure fading into the urban skyline; the space is linear and open, with subtle depth created by receding lines of the platform edges and overhead walkways.
materials: Polished concrete for the platform floor with subtle wear patterns; metal railings and safety fences in brushed stainless steel; glass and metal signage panels; distant structural elements in reinforced concrete and steel beams painted in muted grays; no loose furniture, only fixed architectural features.
lighting: Soft, diffused overhead lighting from station fluorescents and LED strips casting even illumination downward, with subtle shadows from pillar supports; distant city lights from surrounding buildings provide a faint blue-tinged glow on the horizon, maintaining stable and realistic light without harsh contrasts; light direction is primarily top-down with minimal side spill.
atmosphere: A cool, industrial urban ambiance with a sense of vast emptiness and quiet anticipation; low-frequency ambient sounds implied through visual stillness, such as faint echoes on hard surfaces; temperature evokes a crisp, modern metropolitan chill around 15-20°C, with clean air and minimal visual clutter.
establishing_prompt: A wide-angle establishing shot of an empty Tokyo Station platform at the far edge during evening, showing the long concrete expanse receding into the distance under soft overhead fluorescent lights, with metal railings, track rails, and distant arched roof structures against a hazy urban skyline, realistic cinematic style, no people or moving elements, high detail on materials and subtle shadows.
